# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

---
apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: frontend-server
  namespace: frontend
spec:
  port: 8080
  proxy: {}
---
apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: backend-server
  namespace: backend
spec:
  port: 8080
  proxy: {}
---
apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: frontend-to-backend
  namespace: backend
spec:
  server:
    name: backend-server
    namespace: backend
  identity: "frontend.frontend.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"
  port: 8080
---
apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: backend-to-database
  namespace: database
spec:
  server:
    name: database-server
    namespace: database
  serviceAccount: "backend-service-account"
  port: 5432
---
apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: monitoring-to-frontend
  namespace: frontend
spec:
  server:
    name: frontend-server
    namespace: frontend
  identity: "monitoring.monitoring.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"
  port: 8080
---
apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: internal-microservices
  namespace: backend
spec:
  server:
    name: backend-server
    namespace: backend
  serviceAccount: "internal-microservices"
  port: 8080
